item.name: Alert History
item.description: Audit log of alert state transitions and episode pairs

item.name: Get full transition timeline for a single alert

request.name: Get full transition timeline for a single alert

description.content: Returns every state transition for the alert ordered by time. No pagination — the list for a single alert is always small.
Sort order for transitions: ASC (oldest first) or DESC (newest first) (This can only be one of ASC,DESC)

response.name: Timeline returned (may be empty if alert not found or not owned by tenant)

description.content: Sort order for transitions: ASC (oldest first) or DESC (newest first) (This can only be one of ASC,DESC)
Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Count unresolved alerts for a specific sector

request.name: Count unresolved alerts for a specific sector

description.content: Returns the count of currently active (unresolved) alerts in the given sector scoped to the tenant. Eliminates the need for the client to load the full list and filter locally. DESIGN: if the sectorId does not belong to the tenant in the path, this endpoint responds 200 with count=0 (silent denial — does not leak whether the sector exists in another tenant). Callers must NOT use this endpoint to probe sector ownership.

response.name: Count of unresolved alerts (0 if sector not owned by tenant)

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Paginated tenant-wide feed of alert state transitions

request.name: Paginated tenant-wide feed of alert state transitions

description.content: This is the new "Histórico" feed. Each row represents one state change (open or close) across all alerts of the tenant. Supports rich filtering.
Start of the time range (ISO-8601 UTC). Defaults to 30 days ago.
End of the time range (ISO-8601 UTC). Defaults to now.
Filter by source. Comma-separated list of: MQTT, API, SYSTEM.
Filter by source. Comma-separated list of: MQTT, API, SYSTEM.
Filter by severity ID. Comma-separated list.
Filter by severity ID. Comma-separated list.
Filter by alert type ID. Comma-separated list.
Filter by alert type ID. Comma-separated list.
Filter by sector ID. Comma-separated list.
Filter by sector ID. Comma-separated list.
Filter by greenhouse ID. Comma-separated list.
Filter by greenhouse ID. Comma-separated list.
Filter by alert code. Comma-separated list.
Filter by alert code. Comma-separated list.
Filter by actor user ID. Comma-separated list.
Filter by actor user ID. Comma-separated list.
Transition kind filter: ANY, OPEN, CLOSE. (This can only be one of ANY,OPEN,CLOSE)
Start of the time range (ISO-8601 UTC). Defaults to 30 days ago.
End of the time range (ISO-8601 UTC). Defaults to now.
Filter by source. Comma-separated list of: MQTT, API, SYSTEM.
Filter by severity ID. Comma-separated list.
Filter by alert type ID. Comma-separated list.
Filter by sector ID. Comma-separated list.
Filter by greenhouse ID. Comma-separated list.
Filter by alert code. Comma-separated list.
Filter by actor user ID. Comma-separated list.
Transition kind filter: ANY, OPEN, CLOSE. (This can only be one of ANY,OPEN,CLOSE)
Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Paginated list of alert episodes (open→close pairs)

request.name: Paginated list of alert episodes (open→close pairs)

description.content: Each episode represents one activation cycle of an alert. An open episode (not yet closed) will have resolvedAt=null and durationSeconds=null.
Start of the time range (ISO-8601 UTC). Defaults to 30 days ago.
End of the time range (ISO-8601 UTC). Defaults to now.
Filter by severity ID. Comma-separated list.
Filter by severity ID. Comma-separated list.
Filter by sector ID. Comma-separated list.
Filter by sector ID. Comma-separated list.
Filter by alert code. Comma-separated list.
Filter by alert code. Comma-separated list.
When true, only return fully closed episodes (resolvedAt != null).
Start of the time range (ISO-8601 UTC). Defaults to 30 days ago.
End of the time range (ISO-8601 UTC). Defaults to now.
Filter by severity ID. Comma-separated list.

item.name: Rate Limiter
item.description: Gestión del rate limiter de lecturas de sensores

description.content: Pone a cero los contadores de estadísticas
Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

Retorna estadísticas sobre el funcionamiento del rate limiter:
            - enabled: Si está activo
            - minIntervalSeconds: Intervalo mínimo entre lecturas
            - totalReceived: Total de lecturas recibidas
            - totalSaved: Lecturas guardadas en TimescaleDB
            - totalDropped: Lecturas descartadas por rate limiting
            - dropRatePercent: Porcentaje de lecturas descartadas
Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Alert Statistics
item.description: Derived statistics computed from the alert_state_changes audit log

description.content: Returns opened and closed counts per time bucket per group. Use bucket=day for daily charts, bucket=hour for intraday drilldown.
Start of the time range (ISO-8601 UTC). Defaults to 30 days ago.
End of the time range (ISO-8601 UTC). Defaults to now.
Bucket granularity: HOUR, DAY, WEEK, MONTH (This can only be one of HOUR,DAY,WEEK,MONTH)
Grouping dimension: SEVERITY, TYPE (This can only be one of SEVERITY,TYPE)

response.name: List of (bucketStart, key, opened, closed) data points, sorted by time ASC

description.content: Start of the time range (ISO-8601 UTC). Defaults to 30 days ago.
End of the time range (ISO-8601 UTC). Defaults to now.
Bucket granularity: HOUR, DAY, WEEK, MONTH (This can only be one of HOUR,DAY,WEEK,MONTH)
Grouping dimension: SEVERITY, TYPE (This can only be one of SEVERITY,TYPE)
Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

Returns 5 pre-computed summary metrics for the tenant: totalActiveNow, openedToday, closedToday, mttrTodaySeconds, and the top 3 recurring alert codes this week. The from/to parameters currently only affect the top-3 codes sub-query; totalActiveNow and today metrics always use the current UTC day.
Start of the reference time range (ISO-8601 UTC). Defaults to 30 days ago.
End of the reference time range (ISO-8601 UTC). Defaults to now.
Start of the reference time range (ISO-8601 UTC). Defaults to 30 days ago.
End of the reference time range (ISO-8601 UTC). Defaults to now.
Added as a part of security scheme: bearer
Returns alerts ranked by how many times they activated within the time range. Useful for identifying the noisiest alerts.
Start of the time range (ISO-8601 UTC). Defaults to 30 days ago.
End of the time range (ISO-8601 UTC). Defaults to now.
Grouping dimension: CODE, TYPE, SEVERITY, SECTOR, GREENHOUSE (This can only be one of CODE,TYPE,SEVERITY,SECTOR,GREENHOUSE)
Maximum number of buckets to return. Max 100.

response.name: List of recurrence buckets, sorted by count DESC

description.content: Start of the time range (ISO-8601 UTC). Defaults to 30 days ago.
End of the time range (ISO-8601 UTC). Defaults to now.
Grouping dimension: CODE, TYPE, SEVERITY, SECTOR, GREENHOUSE (This can only be one of CODE,TYPE,SEVERITY,SECTOR,GREENHOUSE)
Maximum number of buckets to return. Max 100.
Added as a part of security scheme: bearer
For each group, returns average MTTR and percentiles (p50, p95, p99) in seconds. Only considers fully closed episodes within the time range.
Start of the time range (ISO-8601 UTC). Defaults to 30 days ago.
End of the time range (ISO-8601 UTC). Defaults to now.
Grouping dimension: SEVERITY, TYPE, SECTOR, CODE (This can only be one of SEVERITY,TYPE,SECTOR,CODE)

response.name: List of MTTR buckets, sorted by avg MTTR DESC

description.content: Start of the time range (ISO-8601 UTC). Defaults to 30 days ago.
End of the time range (ISO-8601 UTC). Defaults to now.
Grouping dimension: SEVERITY, TYPE, SECTOR, CODE (This can only be one of SEVERITY,TYPE,SECTOR,CODE)
Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Alert activity ranked by user actor

request.name: Alert activity ranked by user actor

description.content: Shows which users resolved (or opened) the most alerts in the time range. Only USER-kind actors appear; DEVICE and SYSTEM are excluded.
Start of the time range (ISO-8601 UTC). Defaults to 30 days ago.
End of the time range (ISO-8601 UTC). Defaults to now.
Which action to count: RESOLVER (closed alerts) or OPENER (opened alerts) (This can only be one of RESOLVER,OPENER)

response.name: List of per-user activity counts, sorted by count DESC

description.content: Start of the time range (ISO-8601 UTC). Defaults to 30 days ago.
End of the time range (ISO-8601 UTC). Defaults to now.
Which action to count: RESOLVER (closed alerts) or OPENER (opened alerts) (This can only be one of RESOLVER,OPENER)
Added as a part of security scheme: bearer
Sums the duration of all closed episodes per group (code or sector) to show which alerts kept systems degraded the longest.
Start of the time range (ISO-8601 UTC). Defaults to 30 days ago.
End of the time range (ISO-8601 UTC). Defaults to now.
Grouping dimension: CODE, SECTOR (This can only be one of CODE,SECTOR)

response.name: List of active-duration buckets, sorted by total DESC

description.content: Start of the time range (ISO-8601 UTC). Defaults to 30 days ago.
End of the time range (ISO-8601 UTC). Defaults to now.
Grouping dimension: CODE, SECTOR (This can only be one of CODE,SECTOR)
Added as a part of security scheme: bearer
